title: Module: diagnostic & biomarker test appraisal (sensitivity/specificity/PPV)
context: Biomarker-driven oncology trials and diagnostic tests are increasingly prominent, and patient advocates need to understand the performance metrics used to evaluate them: sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value (PPV), negative predictive value (NPV), and how these interact with disease prevalence. This module pairs with a future biomarker worksheet and extends the M1/M2 curriculum. It is patient-facing high-risk content.
objective: Author an original, plain-language educational module on diagnostic and biomarker test appraisal, covering sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, and the effect of prevalence on test performance — for patient advocates reading biomarker-driven trial results.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers: sensitivity and specificity (what each measures), positive and negative predictive value (how they differ from sensitivity/specificity), the effect of disease prevalence on PPV and NPV, and why a highly sensitive test in a low-prevalence population may have low PPV.

Explains the clinical relevance of these concepts to oncology biomarker trials: companion diagnostics, predictive vs prognostic biomarkers, and the difference between analytical validity, clinical validity, and clinical utility.
Uses worked mini-examples with aggregate or synthetic figures only (no individual patient data).
Cites FDA/EMA biomarker validation guidance and authoritative methodological sources; all citations in sources.yaml with compatible allowedUse.
Written in plain language; every statistical term defined on first use.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice and passes the advice-language checklist.
Passes the licence + provenance build gate and CI.
Signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger): clinical framing is correct and nothing reads as diagnostic guidance.
Signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger): content is accessible and does not stray into advice.
Signed off by a methodology reviewer: sensitivity/specificity/PPV explanations and examples are numerically accurate.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Scope — in scope; § Goals — Goal 2)
TASKS.md (Backlog / future — pairs with a biomarker worksheet)
output: A plain-language educational module (content/modules/diagnostic-biomarker-appraisal.md) committed to the repo, covering sensitivity/specificity/PPV/NPV and biomarker test interpretation, provenance-cited, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off plus methodology review.
